name: Horse
description: Anatomy, breeds, terminology, and a few legendary horses — for the bar's resident equestrian.

questions.q: What is the scientific (Linnaean) name for the modern domestic horse?
Which family do horses belong to?

source.notes: Equidae also includes zebras and donkeys.

questions.q: Approximately how long is a horse's pregnancy?
How many bones make up a typical horse's skeleton?
What is the typical lifespan of a well-cared-for horse?
What unit of measurement is used to describe a horse's height?

source.notes: 1 hand = 4 inches (about 10 cm), measured to the withers (top of shoulder).

questions.q: How many inches make up one 'hand' in horse measurement?
Below what height (in hands) is a horse generally classified as a pony?

source.notes: Generally 14.2 hands (58 inches) or under is a pony, with breed-specific exceptions.

questions.q: What is an adult female horse age 4 and over called?
What is a castrated male horse called?
What is a young male horse under four called?

source.notes: A young female under four is a 'filly.'

questions.q: Which breed of horse is famous for its endurance, refined head, and desert origins?

source.notes: One of the oldest breeds; still influences most modern light-horse breeds.

questions.q: Which breed dominates flat horse racing including the Kentucky Derby?
Which large Scottish draft breed is famously associated with Budweiser advertising?
What unique skill lets horses sleep both ways?
choices: They can sleep standing up or lying down

source.original_a: They can sleep both standing and lying down
source.notes: A 'stay apparatus' of tendons in the legs locks them in place to doze upright. They must lie down for REM sleep.

questions.q: What protein is a horse's hoof primarily made of?

source.original_a: Made of keratin, like human fingernails
source.notes: The same protein as human hair and fingernails.

questions.q: What is a foal that has been weaned from its mother but is not yet a year old called?

source.notes: Becomes a 'yearling' on January 1 of the year after birth (in racing parlance).

questions.q: How often do most domestic horses need their hooves trimmed by a farrier?
Which 1973 racehorse won the Triple Crown by an unprecedented 31 lengths at Belmont?

source.notes: Held all three Triple Crown speed records as of 2024.

questions.q: What name is given to the world's oldest recorded horse, who lived to be 62?

source.notes: An English barge horse who died in 1822.
